narrative.headline: 2022 Tesla Model S: forward collision avoidance leads the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 131 complaints with NHTSA. Forward collision avoidance is the most reported area at 25%, followed by steering (15%) and the electrical system (11%). No fires, injuries, or deaths are reported across any area, and only one complaint (in steering) involved a crash. The pattern points to driver-assist behavior rather than hardware failures.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 33 reports (25%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Test the cruise control on the road and watch for unexpected braking.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 20 reports (15%), including the only crash in the data. Try the steering yourself before buying.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 15 reports (11%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Check that controls and switches work reliably.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 11 reports (8%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Several mention hard braking with cruise control engaged.

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: The smallest of the top areas: 9 reports (7%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Several relate to the self-driving feature on highways.
